examples.title: Simple Waste Collection Route Algorithm
examples.description: A basic algorithm demonstrating waste collection route planning
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WasteCollectionRoute
BEGIN
 INPUT: List of collection points with coordinates
 INPUT: Location of disposal site
 
 // Calculate distances between all points
 FOR each point i IN collection_points DO
 FOR each point j IN collection_points DO
 distance[i][j] = CalculateDistance(point[i], point[j])
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 // Nearest neighbor heuristic for route
 current_location = depot
 WHILE unvisited_points > 0 DO
 nearest = FindNearest(current_location, unvisited_points)
 AddToRoute(nearest)
 current_location = nearest
 RemoveFromUnvisited(nearest)
 END WHILE
 
 // Return to depot
 AddToRoute(depot)
 
 OUTPUT: Optimized collection route
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm uses the nearest neighbor heuristic to find an efficient route for waste collection vehicles, minimizing total travel distance.

examples.title: Waste Sorting Classification
examples.description: Algorithm to classify waste into different categories for processing
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WasteClassifier
BEGIN
 INPUT: Waste sample properties
 
 IF waste.composition.organic > 60% THEN
 category = "Compostable"
 ELSE IF waste.recyclable.metals > 10% THEN
 category = "Metal Recycling"
 ELSE IF waste.recyclable.paper > 20% THEN
 category = "Paper Recycling"
 ELSE IF waste.recyclable.plastics > 15% THEN
 category = "Plastic Recycling"
 ELSE IF waste.hazardous = TRUE THEN
 category = "Hazardous Waste"
 ELSE
 category = "Landfill"
 END IF
 
 OUTPUT: Waste category for appropriate processing
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how waste is classified based on composition for appropriate processing and disposal.

examples.title: Landfill Capacity Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to estimate remaining landfill capacity
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateLandfillCapacity
BEGIN
 INPUT: Current waste height H
 INPUT: Maximum allowed height H_max
 INPUT: Landfill surface area A
 INPUT: Compaction ratio R (typical: 3:1)
 
 remaining_height = H_max - H
 available_volume = remaining_height * A
 
 // Account for compaction
 waste_volume = available_volume * R
 
 // Daily waste intake rate
 daily_waste_volume = INPUT daily_waste_tonnage / waste_density
 
 // Days remaining
 days_remaining = waste_volume / daily_waste_volume
 
 OUTPUT: Days until landfill reaches capacity
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates how long a landfill will remain operational based on current waste levels and daily intake.
